title: MAINTENANCE-DEPTH-3
scope: Phase 49/54/70 maintenance + vendor + parts sequel. The scout confirmed the genuine depth-3 gaps: vendor SLA is a VENDOR-SELF view only (VendorSlaService at v/portal/sla) — there is NO landlord-side vendor performance/comparison dashboard or cost-per-ticket analysis; vendor assignment is MANUAL-only (no vendor specialties, no pool suggestion, no auto-route); parts have a single current cost (NO price history, NO supplier-per-part, NO lead-time-aware reorder — parts:reorder-suggest uses a static threshold*2 qty); and photo annotations (Phase 45) are PER-TICKET only (no cross-ticket gallery/export). Reusable seams READY: VendorSlaService + TicketCostService::summarize (cost), SlaDefinitionService cascade, VendorAssignmentService (manual assign + TicketAssignedToVendor event), Part + ticket_parts + parts:reorder-suggest + DraftPurchaseOrder, Document annotates_document_id + TenantScope. 6 categories x 3 = 18 (5H/9M/4L). Hard constraints: TenantScope landlord isolation on every new model/query; vendor.landlord_id === ticket.landlord_id assignment guard preserved; ticket_costs cents-integer money; cron ordering audit-stock → reorder-suggest → audit-sla.

findings.title: Landlord vendor-performance service (compare all vendors: SLA%, resolution, cost/ticket)
findings.evidence: VendorSlaService computes only the calling vendor's own metrics (v/portal/sla). A landlord has no way to compare their vendors to pick the best or control cost.
findings.remediation: VendorPerformanceService::forLandlord(int $landlordId, int $windowDays=90): list of {vendor_id, name, resolved_count, within_sla_pct, avg_resolution_hours, open_overdue, cost_total_cents, cost_per_ticket_cents}. Reuse the VendorSlaService math (extract a shared resolution-window query) + TicketCostService category sums (vendor+parts+labor) per vendor. Strictly landlord-scoped (tickets.landlord_id = $landlordId, vendor.landlord_id match). Phase75 test: two landlords' vendors don't bleed; within_sla% + cost_per_ticket math correct.

findings.title: Landlord vendor-performance dashboard page
findings.remediation: MaintenanceVendorPerformanceController@index → Pages/Maintenance/VendorPerformance.vue: a sortable table (by within-SLA%, cost/ticket, open overdue) + a window selector (30/90/365). Route maintenance.vendor-performance (role:landlord). Link from the Maintenance hub + the assign-vendor UI. data-testid='vendor-performance'. Phase75 feature test: page renders for the landlord with their vendors only.

findings.title: Per-vendor SLA-breach observability + lang + runbook
findings.evidence: tickets:audit-sla emits breach gauges but not dimensioned by vendor; no operator doc for the new dashboard.
findings.remediation: Add a vendor_sla_breach_count{vendor_id} gauge (emitted by a maintenance:vendor-perf-rollup cron OR the existing audit-sla, vendor-dimensioned). lang vendors.performance.*. maintenance.md gains a Phase-70/75 vendor-performance section. Phase75 surface asserts the gauge name + lang keys.

findings.title: Vendor specialties (category competencies)
findings.evidence: Vendor has no specialty/trade columns — a landlord can't record which vendor does plumbing vs electrical, so assignment is blind.
findings.remediation: vendor_specialties table (vendor_id FK cascade, category) unique(vendor_id, category); category validated against Ticket::issueSubcategories() allow-list. Vendor::specialties() relation + sync helper. UI on the vendor edit/create form (checkbox grid of categories). TenantScope via vendor ownership. Phase75 test: specialties persist + are allow-list-gated; cross-tenant vendor rejected.

findings.title: Vendor pool suggestion for a ticket
findings.evidence: The assign-vendor UI shows a flat list; the landlord must know each vendor's trade.
findings.remediation: VendorAssignmentService::suggestPool(Ticket): vendors WHERE landlord match AND is_active AND specialty matches the ticket's subcategory (fallback: all active when none match), ranked by within_sla_pct desc then open-load asc (reuse VendorPerformanceService). Surface 'suggested' vendors at the top of the assign UI with a reason. Phase75 test: pool filters by specialty + ranks by performance.

findings.title: Opt-in auto-route on ticket assignment
findings.evidence: Even with a pool, assignment is a manual click.
findings.remediation: Config/per-landlord opt-in (landlord_hold_settings-style or a maintenance setting) auto_route_vendors. When on, a TicketCreated/assignment listener auto-assigns the top suggestPool vendor (idempotent — only if vendor_id null), via VendorAssignmentService (same guard + TicketAssignedToVendor event with assignment_reason='auto'). Off by default; never overrides a manual assignment. Phase75 test: auto-route assigns the best pool vendor only when enabled; no-op when a vendor is already set or pool empty.

findings.title: Part price history
findings.evidence: Part.cost_per_unit_cents is a single current value; no record of how a part's price changed, so cost trend + audit are impossible.
findings.remediation: part_price_history table (part_id FK cascade, cost_per_unit_cents, source enum {manual,purchase_order,import}, effective_at, recorded_by?). A Part saving observer appends a history row when cost_per_unit_cents changes (+ an initial row on create). PartPriceHistory model (TenantScope via part.landlord_id or landlord_id column). Phase75 test: changing a part's cost appends one history row; unchanged saves don't.

findings.title: Supplier-per-part mapping + comparison
findings.evidence: parts:reorder-suggest infers a vendor from ticket history; there is no real supplier→part catalogue with per-supplier unit cost + lead time.
findings.remediation: part_suppliers table (part_id FK, vendor_id FK, unit_cost_cents, lead_time_days, min_order_qty) unique(part_id, vendor_id); both FKs landlord-scoped. Part::suppliers() relation + cheapestSupplier()/fastestSupplier() helpers. UI to manage suppliers per part. Phase75 test: cheapest/fastest supplier resolution; cross-tenant supplier rejected.

findings.title: Price-history + supplier comparison UI + lang
findings.evidence: The new pricing data needs a surface.
findings.remediation: Parts page gains a per-part price-history sparkline/list + a supplier comparison table (unit cost, lead time, min order). lang parts.pricing.*. Phase75 surface asserts the route/Vue tokens + lang keys.

findings.title: Part usage-rate derivation
findings.evidence: Reorder qty is a static threshold*2 - available; consumption velocity is ignored.
findings.remediation: PartUsageService::dailyRate(Part, int $windowDays=90): sum(ticket_parts.qty_used in window) / windowDays, landlord-scoped. parts_usage_rate_per_day{part_id} gauge from the audit-stock cron. Phase75 test: rate math over a seeded ticket_parts window.

findings.title: Lead-time-aware reorder
findings.evidence: parts:reorder-suggest fires only when qty <= threshold — parts can deplete before a reorder arrives (no lead-time buffer).
findings.remediation: Extend the reorder trigger: order when qty_available <= reorder_threshold + ceil(lead_time_days * dailyRate) (lead_time from the cheapest/known supplier, default config days). Suggested qty grows by the projected lead-time consumption. Reuse Part::belowThreshold by adding a belowEffectiveThreshold scope/helper; parts:reorder-suggest uses it. Preserve idempotent DraftPurchaseOrder upsert + cron ordering. Phase75 test: a part above the static threshold but within the lead-time buffer is suggested; idempotent re-run.

findings.title: Predictive reorder surface + observability + runbook
findings.evidence: The lead-time logic needs visibility + docs.
findings.remediation: Purchase-orders UI shows the trigger reason (static threshold vs lead-time buffer) + projected stockout date. parts_predicted_stockout_count gauge. maintenance.md parts-forecast section. Phase75 surface asserts the gauge + doc.

findings.title: Cross-ticket photo gallery (per building / unit / category)
findings.evidence: Ticket photos + annotations are viewable only inside a single ticket; there is no maintenance photo gallery across tickets.
findings.remediation: MaintenancePhotoGalleryController@index: landlord-scoped query over ticket attachment Documents joined to tickets, filterable by building_id / unit_id / category / date range; groups originals with their annotation siblings (annotates_document_id). Route maintenance.photos (role:landlord, NO N+1). Phase75 test: gallery returns only the landlord's ticket photos, filtered by building + category; signed/streamed image URLs reuse the Phase-59 pattern.

findings.title: Photo gallery Vue page + filters
findings.remediation: Pages/Maintenance/PhotoGallery.vue: a responsive grid with building/category/date filters, original + annotated overlay, lightbox. data-testid='photo-gallery'. Linked from the Maintenance hub. lang maintenance.photos.*. Phase75 surface asserts the route/Vue token + lang.

findings.title: Photo rollup PDF export (contractor handoff / records)
findings.evidence: No way to export a set of ticket photos + annotations for a building/unit/date range.
findings.remediation: MaintenancePhotoGalleryController@exportPdf (dompdf, reuse the Phase-74 export pattern): a PDF of the filtered photo set (original + annotation captions + ticket ref + date), owner-only, throttle:pdf-render. Route maintenance.photos.export-pdf. Phase75 test: owner gets application/pdf; cross-tenant filter yields none / 403.

findings.title: Phase75 cross-category surface + isolation suite
findings.evidence: The new vendor-performance, specialties, auto-route, price-history, supplier, lead-time reorder, and photo gallery are all multi-tenant + money-bearing and must be guarded against drift + cross-tenant regressions.
findings.remediation: Phase75MaintenanceDepth3SurfaceTest: tables (vendor_specialties, part_price_history, part_suppliers) + columns + classes (VendorPerformanceService, PartUsageService, the controllers) + routes (maintenance.vendor-performance, maintenance.photos[.export-pdf], assign-vendor pool) + Vue tokens + lang parity + cron registrations (parts:reorder-suggest lead-time, audit-stock gauges). Behaviour: vendor-perf isolation + cost math, specialty allow-list + cross-tenant, suggestPool ranking, auto-route opt-in, price-history append, supplier cheapest/fastest, lead-time reorder trigger, photo-gallery isolation. All RefreshDatabase + actingAs.

findings.title: Reuse-not-rebuild verification + cron ordering
findings.evidence: Phase 75 extends VendorSlaService/TicketCostService/parts:reorder-suggest/Document rather than rebuilding; the seams + cron ordering must be asserted.
findings.remediation: Assert VendorPerformanceService reuses the VendorSlaService window query (no duplicate SLA math); assert parts:reorder-suggest still runs after parts:audit-stock and before tickets:audit-sla; assert TicketCostService::summarize remains the single cost source. Confirm no N+1 in the photo gallery (NPlusOneBaseline clean).

findings.title: Runbook + nav + lang closeout
findings.evidence: New surfaces need operator docs + navigation + localisation.
findings.remediation: maintenance.md gains vendor-performance + auto-routing + parts-pricing/forecast + photo-gallery sections (incl. the Phase-70 vendor-portal gap noted by the scout). Maintenance hub links to vendor-performance + photo gallery. lang parity finalised (en full; sw/ar [TODO]). Phase75 surface asserts the runbook sections + lang keys.

closeout.summary: Phase 49/54 maintenance-workflow sequel. VENDOR-PERF: VendorPerformanceService::forLandlord (within-SLA %, avg resolution, open overdue, cost/ticket, soft-delete-aware cost join) + sortable /maintenance/vendor-performance. VENDOR-ROUTING: vendor_specialties (allow-list gated to Ticket::issueSubcategories) + suggestPool (specialty filter → performance rank) + opt-in autoAssign (config maintenance.auto_route_vendors, never overrides, TicketObserver afterCommit). PARTS-PRICING: append-only part_price_history (PartObserver on create + cost change only) + part_suppliers (unique part+vendor, landlord-scoped FKs) + cheapest/fastest helpers + /parts/pricing UI (sparkline + supplier comparison). PARTS-PREDICT: PartUsageService dailyRate/dailyRatesFor + effective threshold reorder_threshold+ceil(lead_time*rate); trigger_reason (static|lead_time_buffer) + projected_stockout_at on draft lines; parts_usage_rate_per_day + parts_predicted_stockout_count gauges; coarse DB pre-filter bounds cron memory. PHOTO-ROLLUP: landlord-scoped MaintenancePhotoGalleryController (whereHasMorph ticket filters, originals-only with grouped annotation siblings, no N+1) + grid/lightbox PhotoGallery.vue + dompdf export (capped 60, owner-disk base64, throttle:pdf-render).
closeout.tests: Phase75VendorPerformanceTest + Phase75VendorRoutingTest + Phase75PartsPricingTest (4) + Phase75PartsPredictTest (5) + Phase75PhotoRollupTest (6) + Phase75MaintenanceDepth3SurfaceTest (11). Existing Phase49/Phase54 maintenance suites stay green (no reorder-cron regression).
closeout.constraints_preserved: Every Part/PartSupplier/PartPriceHistory/Vendor/Document/Ticket query landlord-scoped (TenantScope + explicit where); PartSupplierController rejects cross-tenant vendors AND foreign parts; photo gallery + PDF expose only the acting landlord (whereHasMorph runs against the landlord-scoped Ticket subquery); PDF reads only the owner tenant disk; documents.view re-checks ownership; no DB::raw (selectRaw is static SQL only, mirrors VendorPerformanceService); reorder-cron idempotent upsert preserved.
closeout.coderabbit: Clean per sub-phase. Caught/fixed: VENDOR-PERF cost join bypassed SoftDeletes; VENDOR-ROUTING FinanceFilterService::getVendors silently wiped specialties on edit (HIGH); PARTS-PRICING signed-int overflow on cents columns (widened to unsignedBigInteger + validation max) + untranslated flash; PARTS-PREDICT applied LOW-1 coarse pre-filter to bound cron memory; PHOTO-ROLLUP applied LOW-1 (category filter spans issue + complaint subcategories). Cross-tenant/IDOR, Blade XSS, owner-only PDF reads all verified test-backed.
